title: Linear Programming and Constrained Optimization · Assumption Clinic
summary: Choose a fair production and distribution plan under limited resources. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an explainable constrained optimization model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.
Solve two-variable linear programs graphically and verify corner candidates.
Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Optimization Ethics evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Optimization Ethics assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math intermediate optimization. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Optimization Ethics, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an explainable constrained optimization model.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A mathematically optimal plan is automatically fair. This fails the Optimization Ethics contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (3 × 6) − (1 × 2).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (3 × 6) − (1 × 2).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0 of the exact value.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Optimization Ethics applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Objective Constraints to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Objective Constraints.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using evaluate and whose plus specific case evidence.
